description_plain: Your child can create a probability model by doing a chance activity many times and recording what happens.

subject: Math
description: Develop a uniform probability model by assigning equal probability to all outcomes, and use the model to determine probabilities of events.
description_plain: Your child can make a simple probability model where all outcomes are equally likely and use it to find chances of events.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model and use it to find probabilities of simple events.
description_plain: Your child can create a probability model and use it to find chances of simple events.

subject: Math
description: Collect data to calculate the experimental probability of a chance event, observing its long-run relative frequency. Use this experimental probability to predict the approximate relative frequency.
description_plain: Your child can collect data from chance events to find experimental probabilities and use them to predict future results.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1 that expresses the likelihood of the event occurring.
description_plain: Your child understands that probability is a number between 0 and 1 that shows how likely an event is to happen.

subject: Math
description: Use measures of center and measures of variability for numerical data from random samples to draw comparative inferences about two populations.
description_plain: Your child can use averages and measures of spread to compare two groups of numerical data.

subject: Math
description: Informally assess the difference between two data sets by examining the overlap and separation between the graphical representations of two data sets.
description_plain: Your child can look at graphs of two data sets to see how much they overlap or differ.

subject: Math
description:
Calculate the measure of variability of a data set and understand that it describes how the values of the data set vary with a single number.
  • Understand the mean absolute deviation of a data set is a measure of variability that describes the average distance that points within a data set are from the mean of the data set.
  • Understand that the range describes the spread of the entire data set.
  • Understand that the interquartile range describes the spread of the middle 50% of the data.
description_plain: Your child can calculate how much data values vary using range, mean absolute deviation, and interquartile range.

subject: Math
description: Recognize the role of variability when comparing two populations.
description_plain: Your child understands why variability matters when comparing two groups of data.

subject: Math
description: Generate multiple random samples (or simulated samples) of the same size to gauge the variation in estimates or predictions, and use this data to draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic of interest.
description_plain: Your child can take many random samples to see how estimates change and use this to learn about a larger group.

subject: Math
description:
Understand that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by:
  • Recognizing that generalizations about a population from a sample are valid only if the sample is representative of that population.
  • Using random sampling to produce representative samples to support valid inferences.
description_plain: Your child understands that we can learn about a whole group by studying a random sample that fairly represents it.

subject: Math
description:
Construct inequalities to solve problems by reasoning about the quantities.
  • Fluently solve multi-step inequalities with the variable on one side, including those generated by word problems.
  • Compare an algebraic solution process for equations and an algebraic solution process for inequalities.
  • Graph the solution set of the inequality and interpret in context.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve inequalities from problems, graph their solutions, and explain what they mean.

subject: Math
description:
Construct equations to solve problems by reasoning about the quantities.
  • Fluently solve multistep equations with the variable on one side, including those generated by word problems.
  • Compare an algebraic solution to an arithmetic solution, identifying the sequence of the operations used in each approach.
  • Interpret the solution in context.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve equations from problems and explain their answers.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent quantities to solve real-world or mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description:
Solve multi-step real-world and mathematical problems posed with rational numbers in algebraic expressions.
  • Apply properties of operations to calculate with positive and negative numbers in any form.
  • Convert between different forms of a number and equivalent forms of the expression as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems with positive and negative numbers and change numbers between different forms.

subject: Math
description: Understand that equivalent expressions can reveal real-world and mathematical relationships. Interpret the meaning of the parts of each expression in context.
description_plain: Your child understands that different but equal expressions can show real-life relationships and can explain parts of those expressions.

subject: Math
description:
Apply properties of operations as strategies to:
  • Add, subtract, and expand linear expressions with rational coefficients.
  • Factor linear expression with an integer GCF.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, expand, and factor expressions with numbers and letters.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving numerical expressions with rational numbers using the four operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description:
Use division and previous understandings of fractions and decimals.
  • Convert a fraction to a decimal using long division.
  • Understand that the decimal form of a rational number terminates in 0s or eventually repeats.
description_plain: Your child can change fractions to decimals using long division and knows decimals either end or repeat.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies, including the standard algorithms, to multiply and divide rational numbers and describe the product and quotient in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide rational numbers and explain what the answers mean in real life.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a rational number is any number that can be written as a quotient of integers with a non-zero divisor.
description_plain: Your child understands that rational numbers are numbers that can be written as fractions with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide rational numbers using what they already know.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of addition and subtraction to add and subtract rational numbers, using the properties of operations, and describing real-world contexts using sums and differences.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract rational numbers and explain real-life situations using these operations.

subject: Math
description: Use scale factors and unit rates in proportional relationships to solve ratio and percent problems.
description_plain: Your child can use scale factors and unit rates to solve problems with ratios and percentages.

subject: Math
description:
Use a graphical representation of a proportional relationship in context to:
  • Explain the meaning of any point (x, y).
  • Explain the meaning of (0, 0) and why it is included.
  • Understand that the y-coordinate of the ordered pair (1, r) corresponds to the unit rate and explain its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain what points on a graph of a proportional relationship mean, including the special points (0,0) and (1, unit rate).

subject: Math
description: Create equations and graphs to represent proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can make equations and graphs to show proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Identify the unit rate (constant of proportionality) within two quantities in a proportional relationship using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions.
description_plain: Your child can find the unit rate in proportional relationships using tables, graphs, equations, or words.

subject: Math
description:
Understand that a proportion is a relationship of equality between ratios.
  • Represent proportional relationships using tables and graphs.
  • Recognize whether ratios are in a proportional relationship using tables and graphs.
  • Compare two different proportional relationships using tables, graphs, equations, and verbal descriptions.
description_plain: Your child understands that proportions show equal ratios and can use tables and graphs to identify and compare them.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and show proportional relationships between quantities.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of fractions to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find unit rates from ratios with fractions to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description:
Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving:
  • Area and perimeter of two-dimensional objects composed of triangles, quadrilaterals, and polygons.
  • Volume and surface area of pyramids, prisms, or three-dimensional objects composed of cubes, pyramids, and right prisms.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about area, perimeter, volume, and surface area of shapes like triangles, polygons, pyramids, and prisms.

subject: Math
description: Use facts about supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles in a multi-step problem to write and solve equations for an unknown angle in a figure.
description_plain: Your child can use facts about different types of angles to write and solve equations for unknown angles in shapes.

subject: Math
description:
Understand area and circumference of a circle.
  • Understand the relationships between the radius, diameter, circumference, and area.
  • Apply the formulas for area and circumference of a circle to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child understands and can use formulas for the area and circumference of circles to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand the characteristics of angles and side lengths that create a unique triangle, more than one triangle or no triangle. Build triangles from three measures of angles and/or sides.
description_plain: Your child can tell when three angles or sides make one triangle, more than one, or none, and can build triangles from given measures.

subject: Math
description:
Solve problems involving scale drawings of geometric figures by:
  • Building an understanding that angle measures remain the same and side lengths are proportional.
  • Using a scale factor to compute actual lengths and areas from a scale drawing.
  • Creating a scale drawing.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with scale drawings by understanding that angles stay the same and sides change by a scale factor, and can make scale drawings.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description:
Analyze center and variability by:
  • Giving quantitative measures of center, describing variability, and any overall pattern, and noting any striking deviations.
  • Justifying the appropriate choice of measures of center using the shape of the data distribution.
description_plain: Your child can describe the center, spread, and overall pattern of data, explain any unusual points, and choose the best way to show the center based on the data shape.

subject: Math
description:
Describe the collected data by:
  • Reporting the number of observations in dot plots and histograms.
  • Communicating the nature of the attribute under investigation, how it was measured, and the units of measurement.
description_plain: Your child can explain data by counting how many observations there are in dot plots and histograms and by describing what was measured and the units used.

subject: Math
description: Summarize numerical data sets in relation to their context.
description_plain: Your child can summarize sets of numbers by explaining what they mean in the situation they come from.

subject: Math
description:
Display numerical data in plots on a number line.
  • Use dot plots, histograms, and box plots to represent data.
  • Compare the attributes of different representations of the same data.
description_plain: Your child can show numbers on a number line using dot plots, histograms, and box plots, and compare how these different pictures show the same data.

subject: Math
description: Understand that describing the variability of a data set is needed to distinguish between data sets in the same scale, by comparing graphical representations of different data sets in the same scale that have similar measures of center, but different spreads.
description_plain: Your child understands that looking at how spread out data is helps tell data sets apart, even when their middle values are similar.

subject: Math
description:
Determine the measure of center of a data set and understand that it is a single number that summarizes all the values of that data set.
  • Understand that a mean is a measure of center that represents a balance point or fair share of a data set and can be influenced by the presence of extreme values within the data set.
  • Understand the median as a measure of center that is the numerical middle of an ordered data set.
description_plain: Your child can find the middle value (median) and the average (mean) of a data set and knows the mean can be affected by very high or low numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that both a measure of center and a description of variability should be considered when describing a numerical data set.
description_plain: Your child understands that to describe a set of numbers well, you need to talk about both the middle value and how spread out the numbers are.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a set of data collected to answer a statistical question has a distribution which can be described by its center, spread, and overall shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that data collected to answer a question can be described by its middle, how spread out it is, and its overall shape.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child can recognize questions that expect different answers because the data can vary.

subject: Math
description: Represent right prisms and right pyramids using nets made up of rectangles and triangles, and use the nets to find the surface area of these figures. Apply these techniques in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can draw flat patterns (nets) made of rectangles and triangles to build right prisms and pyramids and use these to find their surface area in real problems.

subject: Math
description:
Use the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems by:
  • Drawing polygons in the coordinate plane given coordinates for the vertices.
  • Using coordinates to find the length of a side joining points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can draw shapes on a coordinate grid using points and find the length of sides when points share an x- or y-coordinate.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of the volume of a right rectangular prism to find the volume of right rectangular prisms with fractional edge lengths. Apply this understanding to the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes with fractional side lengths and use this skill to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description:
Create geometric models to solve real-world and mathematical problems to:
  • Find the area of triangles by composing into rectangles and decomposing into right triangles.
  • Find the area of special quadrilaterals and polygons by decomposing into triangles or rectangles.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by making shapes to find areas of triangles and special polygons by breaking them into rectangles and triangles.

subject: Math
description:
Represent and analyze quantitative relationships by:
  • Using variables to represent two quantities in a real-world or mathematical context that change in relationship to one another.
  • Analyze the relationship between quantities in different representations (context, equations, tables, and graphs).
description_plain: Your child can use variables to show how two quantities change together and understand their relationship using equations, tables, and graphs.

subject: Math
description:
Reason about inequalities by:
  • Using substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an inequality true.
  • Writing an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem.
  • Recognizing that inequalities of the form x > c or x < c have infinitely many solutions.
  • Representing solutions of inequalities on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can check if numbers make inequalities true, write inequalities to show limits in problems, know these have many solutions, and show solutions on number lines.

subject: Math
description:
Solve real-world and mathematical problems by writing and solving equations of the form:
  • x + p = q in which p, q and x are all non-negative rational numbers; and,
  • p · x = q for cases in which p, q and x are all non-negative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by writing and solving simple equations like x plus a number equals another number, or a number times x equals another number.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent numbers and write expressions when solving a real-world or mathematical problem.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers and write expressions to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation true.
description_plain: Your child can check if a number makes an equation true by substituting it in.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent and justify with mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two expressions mean the same thing and explain why.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions without exponents.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to rewrite expressions in different but equal ways without using exponents.

subject: Math
description:
Write, read, and evaluate algebraic expressions.
  • Write expressions that record operations with numbers and with letters standing for numbers.
  • Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms and view one or more of those parts as a single entity.
  • Evaluate expressions at specific values of their variables using expressions that arise from formulas used in real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and solve expressions with numbers and letters, name parts of expressions, and find their value for given numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions, with and without grouping symbols, involving whole-number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve number expressions with whole-number exponents, with or without parentheses.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of addition and subtraction.Describe situations in which opposite quantities combine to make 0.Understand p + q as the number located a distance q from p, in the positive or negative direction depending on the sign of q. Show that a number and its additive inverse create a zero pair.Understand subtraction of integers as adding the additive inverse, p - q = p + (-q). Show that the distance between two integers on the number line is the absolute value of their difference.Use models to add and subtract integers from -20 to 20 and describe real-world contexts using sums and differences.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract integers from -20 to 20 using models, understand opposite numbers cancel out to zero, and explain real-life situations using these sums and differences.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Include use of coordinates and absolute value to find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four parts of the coordinate plane and find distances between points that share an x- or y-coordinate.

subject: Math
description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain order statements about rational numbers in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret statements of inequality as statements about the relative position of two numbers on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can understand inequality statements as showing which number is bigger or smaller on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand ordering of rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how to order rational numbers from smallest to largest.

subject: Math
description:
On a coordinate plane:
  • Understand signs of numbers in ordered pairs as indicating locations in quadrants.
  • Recognize that when two ordered pairs differ only by signs, the locations of the points are related by reflections across one or both axes.
  • Find and position pairs of rational numbers on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can find and place points with positive and negative numbers on a coordinate plane, understand reflections across axes, and know what signs tell about location.

subject: Math
description:
On a number line:
  • Recognize opposite signs of numbers as indicating locations on opposite sides of 0 and that the opposite of the opposite of a number is the number itself.
  • Find and position rational numbers on a horizontal or vertical number line.
description_plain: Your child can find and place rational numbers on a number line, understand that opposite signs mean opposite sides of zero, and know that the opposite of the opposite number is the original number.

subject: Math
description: Understand rational numbers as points on the number line and as ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child understands rational numbers as points on number lines and as pairs of numbers on coordinate planes.

subject: Math
description:
Understand and use rational numbers to:
  • Describe quantities having opposite directions or values.
  • Represent quantities in real-world contexts, explaining the meaning of 0 in each situation.
  • Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line to:
    • Interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world context.
    • Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.
description_plain: Your child can use rational numbers to describe opposite directions or values, explain what zero means in different situations, and understand absolute value as distance from zero.

subject: Math
description:
Understand and use prime factorization and the relationships between factors to:
  • Find the unique prime factorization for a whole number.
  • Find the greatest common factor of two whole numbers less than or equal to 100.
  • Use the greatest common factor and the distributive property to rewrite the sum of two whole numbers, each less than or equal to 100.
  • Find the least common multiple of two whole numbers less than or equal to 12 to add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators.
description_plain: Your child can find the prime factors of whole numbers, find the greatest common factor and least common multiple of numbers, and use these to add or subtract fractions with different denominators.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of decimals to develop and fluently use the standard algorithms for addition, subtraction, multiplication and division of decimals.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals using standard methods and understand how to use them fluently.

subject: Math
description: Fluently divide using long division with a minimum of a four-digit dividend and interpret the quotient and remainder in context.
description_plain: Your child can do long division with numbers of at least four digits and understand what the answer and remainder mean in real problems.

subject: Math
description:
Use visual models and common denominators to:
  • Interpret and compute quotients of fractions.
  • Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving division of fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and common denominators to divide fractions and solve problems involving fraction division.

subject: Math
description:
Use ratio reasoning to solve real-world and mathematical problems with percents by:
  • Understanding and finding a percent of a quantity as a ratio per 100.
  • Using equivalent ratios, such as benchmark percents (50%, 25%, 10%, 5%, 1%), to determine a part of any given quantity.
  • Finding the whole, given a part and the percent.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems with percents by understanding percents as parts per 100, using common percents like 50% or 25%, and finding the whole when given a part and a percent.

subject: Math
description:
Use ratio reasoning with equivalent whole-number ratios to solve real-world and mathematical problems by:
  • Creating and using a table to compare ratios.
  • Finding missing values in the tables.
  • Using a unit ratio.
  • Converting and manipulating measurements using given ratios.
  • Plotting the pairs of values on the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can use tables to compare ratios, find missing numbers, use unit ratios, convert measurements, and plot ratio pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Understand that ratios can be expressed as equivalent unit ratios by finding and interpreting both unit ratios in context.
description_plain: Your child understands that ratios can be written as unit ratios and can find and explain these in real situations.

subject: Math
description:
Understand the concept of a ratio and use ratio language to:
  • Describe a ratio as a multiplicative relationship between two quantities.
  • Model a ratio relationship using a variety of representations.
description_plain: Your child understands what a ratio is and can describe it as a relationship between two quantities using different ways to show it.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; develop vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly and can research words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference in feelings or ideas between words that have similar dictionary meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech in context based on grade 6 reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like metaphors and similes based on what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, parts of the word, word relationships, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same style and tone when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentence types to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules of language when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 6-8 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 6-8 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas clearly and in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child identifies a speaker’s main argument and knows which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand information shown in different ways, like videos or charts, and explain how it helps explain a topic or issue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain different viewpoints by summarizing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions to add to a discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, sets goals and deadlines, and knows their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying, and uses facts from that preparation to ask questions and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one on one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks about grade 6 topics, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and websites, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying, giving credit to the sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the investigation when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and changing their focus if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient word processing skills.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, publish their work, and work with others, showing they know how to use word processing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to make it better for the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense based on the story or events they told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, details, and descriptions that appeal to the senses to show experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and when things change in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, and description to develop events, experiences, or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters or narrator, and organize events in a clear, natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize their ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to fit the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that follows from the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style that fits the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use transition words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can develop a topic by adding facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting, graphics, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and organize ideas using ways like definition, comparison, or cause and effect, and use pictures or multimedia to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to fit the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child can write using a formal style that fits the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child can support their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their claims and organize reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to get ready to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 6, read and understand informational texts within the 6-8 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand informational texts for their grade level on their own and connect what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two different authors tell about the same events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas, and tell which ideas are not supported.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from pictures, numbers, and words to understand a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text and explain how a word choice changes the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an important person, event, or idea is introduced and described with examples in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the text to support what the text says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories for their grade level on their own and connect what they read to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message of a text, explain how details show it, and summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a story, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the story or poem to support what it says clearly and what can be understood by thinking about it.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the story says and make logical guesses, using examples from the story to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; develop vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-appropriate academic and subject-specific words and can research new words on their own.

description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like metaphors or idioms, in the context of what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.

description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words using clues from the text, word parts, and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child chooses words that clearly and briefly express ideas, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 6-8 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 6-8 grammar continuum.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child can present ideas clearly and with important details, using good vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas and details from different kinds of media and how they help understand a topic or issue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and the internet, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.

subject: Language Arts
