subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and explain how the author shows their opinion is different from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how the main sections help develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text and explain how specific word choices affect the meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how people, events, and ideas interact in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the text to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 7, read and understand literature within the 6-8 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: By the end of 7th grade, your child can read and understand literature independently and connect it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, video, or live version and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the perspectives of different characters in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' viewpoints and how they contrast.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's (or other literary genre's) form or structure contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the structure or form of a poem, drama, or story adds to its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text; analyze the impact of rhymes and repetitions of sounds on meaning and tone in a specific line or section of a literary work.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a story or poem and explain how rhymes and repeated sounds affect meaning and tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a story affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how parts of a story or play work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the main message or theme of a story and explains how it develops, then summarizes the story clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a story, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the story or play to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the story says and make logical guesses, using examples from the story to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words and phrases that fit their grade level, including words that show location and time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and non-literal meanings of words and phrases in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand when words or phrases mean exactly what they say and when they mean something else.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using clues from the text, word parts, word relationships, or reference tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and understand the differences between how people speak and how they write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 2-3 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 2-3 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child makes interesting audio recordings of stories or poems that are easy to understand and adds pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly in complete sentences at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what a speaker says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details when listening to stories or information in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand what others say, stay focused on the topic, and connect their ideas to what others have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child can follow agreed-upon rules when having discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying the material and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, build on their ideas, and clearly share their own thoughts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from experiences or find it in books and online, take short notes, and sort facts into categories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing (using word processing skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers and digital tools to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing, with consideration to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child can improve their writing by revising and editing to fit the task and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Provide a sense of closure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal transition words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words that show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to show events and how characters respond.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting up the situation and introducing characters, then tell events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to get ready to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events with clear details and a clear sequence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing, with consideration to task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by revising and editing with help from others, keeping the purpose in mind.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'also' or 'for example' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, and details to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and groups related information together, using pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic clearly and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing, with consideration to task and purpose.
description_plain: Your child will make their writing better by revising and editing with help from others, focusing on the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear conclusion to finish their opinion piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses linking words to connect their opinion and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states an opinion, and lists reasons in an organized way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that share their point of view and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the story to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level words that are spelled in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllabic words.
description_plain: Decode multisyllabic words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read words at grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create readable documents with legible handwriting (manuscript and cursive).
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and neatly by hand, using both print and cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 3, read and understand informational texts at the high end of the 2-3 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand harder informational texts on their own and connect what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts the main points and details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how the author connects ideas between sentences and paragraphs to support specific points in a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how the author links ideas between sentences and paragraphs to make points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in a text to show they understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses features like headings and search tools to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child describes how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected using words about time and cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, tell important details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read and understand harder stories on their own and connect them to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell how themes, settings, and plots are alike or different in stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part adds to the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, identifying words that impact the meaning in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a story affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child describes characters and explains how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories like fables and myths from different cultures and explain the lesson or message using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a story, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the story says and make logical guesses, using examples from the story to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; develop vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words for school subjects and topics, and looks up words on their own to understand them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech in context based on grade 8 reading and content.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech like metaphors and similes in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words using clues, word parts, relationships, or dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the active or passive voice and in the conditional mood to achieve particular effects.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in active or passive voice and conditional mood to create effects in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 6-8 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 6-8 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make ideas clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child presents ideas clearly and confidently, using good reasons, details, and appropriate words, while making eye contact and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence, and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child explains a speaker’s argument and checks if the reasons and evidence make sense and if anything irrelevant is included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats and evaluate the motives behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child analyzes why information is presented in different media and thinks about the reasons behind it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes or explains their own ideas based on the evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that connect ideas from different speakers and responds with relevant facts and thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group discussions, helps make decisions, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and knows their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to explore and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one on one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 8 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child participates well in discussions with others, sharing ideas clearly and building on what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child gathers information from many sources, checks if they are trustworthy, and uses the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new related questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools to write, publish, and share ideas clearly and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by revising and editing with some help, focusing on the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words, detailed descriptions, and sensory language to show actions and share experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence, signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another, and show the relationships among experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child will use different transition words and phrases to show the order of events, changes in time or place, and how experiences are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child can use storytelling techniques like dialogue, pacing, description, and reflection to develop events, experiences, or characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child will start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters or a narrator, and organize events in a natural, logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, clear details, and well-organized events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help from others, your child will improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or section that supports and finishes the information or explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write using a formal style appropriate for the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and exact words, including subject-specific vocabulary, to explain or inform about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child will use different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing clear and easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that relate well to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories; include formatting, graphics, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will introduce a topic clearly, organize ideas into groups, and use headings, charts, or pictures if needed to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear informational texts that explain ideas and facts about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help from others, your child will improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing statement or section that supports and finishes their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child will write using a formal style appropriate for the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to connect ideas and show how claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child will support their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their claims, explain how they differ from other views, and organize reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that clearly support their claims with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and understand informational texts at the high end of the 6-8 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand challenging informational texts on their own for long periods, connecting what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts that disagree on facts or ideas and explain where they differ.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; recognize when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can find and evaluate the main argument and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense and are relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child can think about the good and bad points of using different ways to share information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author acknowledges and responds to conflicting evidence or viewpoints.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail the structure of a specific paragraph in a text, including the role of particular sentences in developing and refining a key concept.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a paragraph is organized and how its sentences help develop a main idea.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text, including its relationship to supporting ideas; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how it develops, and summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

description: Cite textual evidence that most strongly supports an analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases learned through conversations, reading, and being read to, including common conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening, including common connecting words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner and adjectives differing in intensity by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs and adjectives by defining, choosing, or acting out their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes.
description_plain: Your child can define words by naming their category and important features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups to understand what the groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child can understand small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies: context clues, word parts and word relationships.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, word parts, or how words relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2
description_plain: Begins in grade 2

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the K-1 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the K-1 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas, thoughts, and feelings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences to describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child can say full sentences to describe people, places, things, and events with details, sharing ideas and feelings clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand better when something is confusing during talks about stories or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child will join conversations by listening and responding to what others say, having back-and-forth talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child can follow agreed rules when joining group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child will take part in group talks with classmates and adults about first-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: Your child can work together with others on research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child will use computers or tablets with help from adults to write and share their work, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can stay focused on a topic, listen to friends’ ideas, and add details to make their writing better with adult help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child will organize their thoughts and information about a topic to get ready to write, with adult support.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal transition words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that tell about two or more events in order, include details, use words that show time order, and finish the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can focus on a topic, respond to friends’ questions and ideas, and add details to improve their writing with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child will organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing, with adult guidance.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide closure.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by naming it, giving some facts, and ending the writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can focus on a topic, listen to friends’ suggestions, and add details to make their writing stronger with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child will organize information and ideas about a topic to plan what to write, with adult support.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide closure.
description_plain: Your child can write their opinion by saying the topic or book, giving a reason for their opinion, and ending their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.
