subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the story to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child reads words that have endings like -s, -ed, or -ing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child reads two-syllable words by breaking them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows how the letter 'e' at the end and vowel pairs show long vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child reads one-syllable words that follow common spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how certain letter pairs (like 'sh' or 'ch') make specific sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can break spoken one-syllable words into all their individual sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say whole one-syllable words by putting together individual sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lower-case letters legibly.
description_plain: Your child can write all uppercase and lowercase letters clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use capitalization and ending punctuation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use capital letters and ending punctuation like periods and question marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of books and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and understand informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1 for sustained periods of time.
description_plain: With help, your child can read and understand simple informational books for their grade for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find basic similarities and differences between two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can find reasons the author gives to support ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use parts of a book, like headings or pictures, to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic and important details from a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and understand literature of appropriate complexity for grade 1 for sustained periods of time.
description_plain: With help, your child can read and understand stories and poems for their grade for longer times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can tell how characters’ adventures and experiences are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a story to describe characters, places, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between storybooks and books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help imagine senses like sight or sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a story affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, places, and important events in a story using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories with important details and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a story, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the story says and make logical guesses, using examples from the story to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that show ideas like contrast and addition, such as however, although, and moreover.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language (like similes and metaphors) and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using clues from the sentence, word parts, or dictionaries.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can compare different types of English used in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or clearer to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 4-5 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 4-5 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on skills learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks.
description_plain: Your child can report on a topic or share an opinion clearly, using facts and details in order, and speak at a pace that is easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child summarizes what a speaker says and explains how the speaker supports their ideas with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from a text read aloud or from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas shared in a discussion and can make conclusions based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions during a discussion and add to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the agreed rules during discussions and does the roles they are assigned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying the topic and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of discussions with others, listens to their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find information from books or the internet, summarize it in notes or writing, and list where they found it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of word processing skills.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use computers to write, share their work, and work with others, showing good typing skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas with help from others, keeping in mind who they are writing for and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that clearly wraps up the story or events they have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words and details that help readers picture and understand their experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child includes dialogue, descriptions, and pacing to make stories and characters more interesting and real.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas and information about a topic to get ready to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas with help from others, keeping in mind who they are writing for and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a concluding sentence or section that relates to the information or explanation they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses.
description_plain: Your child connects ideas within and between groups of information using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, or examples to explain their topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting, illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, shares a general idea, and organizes related information in a logical way, using pictures or media when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas with help from others, keeping in mind who they are writing for and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses.
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a logical order and supports them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child clearly introduces a topic or text, states their opinion, and organizes ideas logically to support their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child organizes ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces on topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new multisyllable words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create readable documents through legible handwriting (cursive).
description_plain: Your child writes neatly in cursive so others can read their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 5, read and understand informational texts at the high end of the 4-5 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: By the end of 5th grade, your child can read and understand challenging informational texts on their own for a long time, connecting what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different books or articles about the same topic and use it to talk or write clearly about that topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas and can point out which reasons go with which ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can find answers or solve problems quickly by using information from several books, websites, or other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can look at different stories about the same event and notice important similarities and differences in how people see it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts how two or more texts organize events, ideas, or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to school subjects or topics they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more people, events, or ideas are connected based on details in a history, science, math, or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a text accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 5, read and understand literature at the high end of the 4-5 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: By the end of 5th grade, your child can read and understand challenging stories and literature on their own for a long time, connecting what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts stories in the same type of literature by looking at how they handle similar themes and topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or aesthetics of a text.
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures, videos, or other media add meaning, mood, or style to a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child understands how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes the way events are described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child explains how parts like chapters, scenes, or stanzas help organize a story, play, or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, recognizing specific word choices that contribute to meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text and notices how word choices affect meaning and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a story affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more characters, places, or events in a story by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem by looking at details and can tell the story in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a story, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a story or poem accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the story says and make logical guesses, using examples from the story to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases learned through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and responding to texts, including describing with adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs and closely related adjectives.
description_plain: Your child notices small differences in meaning between similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child figures out or clears up the meaning of unknown or tricky words using clues from the text, word parts, word relationships, or reference tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 2-3 conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 2-3 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent and complete sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience using facts and details, speaking clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation when they don’t understand what is being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child follows agreed-upon rules when having discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds information from given sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: Your child takes part in group research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child uses different digital tools to create and share writing, sometimes working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by revising and editing with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas about a topic to plan their writing with help from adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal transition words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about events with details about actions and feelings, use words that show order, and end with a closing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can focus on a topic and make their writing better by revising and editing with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas about a topic to plan their writing with help from adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and definitions, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: Your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by revising and editing with help from adults and friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas about a topic to plan their writing with help from adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that state a topic and opinion, give reasons, use linking words, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the story to figure out or fix words they don’t know by rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can identify words that have unusual but common spelling and sound patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common beginnings and endings like prefixes and suffixes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel teams that make common sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word parts to figure out how to read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lower-case letters legibly and proportionally.
description_plain: Your child can write all uppercase and lowercase letters neatly and in the right size.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 2, read and understand informational texts within the 2-3 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand informational books for their grade level on their own and connect what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the main points from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures help make the text clearer and easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the author's main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main reason why an author wrote a text, like what they want to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like headings and captions to quickly find important facts or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in texts about grade 2 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the main topic of a text and the focus of each paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and poems for their grade level on their own and connect what they read to what they already know.

description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from adults and/or peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from adults and/or peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
