description_plain: Your child knows the main sounds that letters make and can say many of the common sounds for each consonant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use letter sounds and word parts to figure out how to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change or add sounds in simple one-syllable words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in simple three-letter words like 'cat' or 'dog'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can put together and break apart the beginning and ending sounds in one-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, break apart, and put together the beats (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how spoken words are made up of smaller sounds and parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print upper- and lower-case letters.
description_plain: Your child can write both uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lower-case letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name all the uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that words are separated by spaces when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words match specific letters in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right and top to bottom.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and what it looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can join group reading activities and understand what is being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: With help, your child can find ways two books about the same topic are alike and different, like in pictures or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 1.
description_plain: Begins in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe how the words and illustrations work together to provide information.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures and words work together to give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, define the role of the author and illustrator in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell what the author and illustrator do to share ideas or information in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic of a book and share important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group reading and understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell how characters in stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe how the words and illustrations work together to tell a story.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures and words work together to tell a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, define the role of the author and illustrator in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell what the author and illustrator do to tell a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts.
description_plain: Your child can recognize common kinds of books and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about words in a text that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words in a story that show feelings or describe what things look, sound, or feel like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a story affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is in the story, where it happens, and what big things happen, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: Your child can retell familiar stories and include important details, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a story, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the story says and make logical guesses, using examples from the story to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being and that are basic to a particular topic.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words that are important for school subjects and that show clear actions, feelings, or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in developing vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words by knowing their opposites and similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child understands common sayings and explains what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain what simple comparisons like similes and metaphors mean when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands when words are used in special ways and can notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain phrases that don't mean exactly what the words say, like idioms or metaphors, and notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies: context clues, word parts, word relationships, and reference materials.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, parts of the word, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine and/or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, word relationships, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can understand new or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking down word parts, seeing how words relate, or checking dictionaries and other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is appropriate.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English and when it’s okay to speak more casually.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child chooses punctuation to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when they write, speak, read, or listen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the 4-5 conventions continuum
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band conventions continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the 4-5 grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; demonstrate proficiency within the appropriate grade band grammar continuum.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or videos to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; adjust speech as appropriate to formal and informal discourse.
description_plain: Your child can clearly tell about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can share information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas, using the right style and organization for the topic and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons and proof a speaker gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and understanding after listening to a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to understand better and add helpful comments during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during discussions and do their assigned roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find information from books or the internet, take notes, organize the facts, and list where the information came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are reliable and accurate, and use the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects based on clear questions and show they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of word processing skills.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use computers or tablets to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use digital tools and resources to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can improve their writing by making changes and fixing mistakes to better fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that makes sense based on the story or events they told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that help the reader picture what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and descriptions to show what happened or how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tell what happens in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child can organize ideas and information about a topic before starting to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined experiences using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: With help, your child can improve their writing by revising and editing to make it better for the reader and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words to explain or give information about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child connects ideas within the same topic using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, examples, or quotes to explain the topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting, illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic and group related information into paragraphs, using pictures or other media to help explain when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child will organize ideas and information about a topic to get ready to write.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing, with consideration to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by revising and editing with help from others, thinking about the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child will write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using linking words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons supported by facts and details to explain their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child will clearly introduce a topic or text, state an opinion, and organize ideas to support their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize information and ideas around a topic to plan and prepare to write.
description_plain: Your child will organize ideas and information about a topic to plan their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text to check and fix how they read words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new multi-syllable words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create readable documents through legible handwriting (cursive).
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and neatly using cursive handwriting.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 4, read and understand informational texts within the 4-5 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand informational texts at their grade level on their own for longer periods, connecting what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain how pictures, spoken information, or data help explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and explain how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or information are organized in a text or part of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a text affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain events or ideas in history, science, or technical texts, including what happened and why, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how key details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas (RI) or themes (RL) of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 4, read and understand literature within the 4-5 text complexity band proficiently and independently for sustained periods of time. Connect prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and literature at their grade level on their own for longer periods, connecting what they read to what they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and understand complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently, connecting prior knowledge and experiences to text.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own, connecting it to their own experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the use of similar themes and topics and patterns of events in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast similar themes, topics, and event patterns in stories, myths, and traditional tales from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play to its visual or oral version, pointing out where they match.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare different points of view in stories, like first-person versus third-person narration.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view, perspective, or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems and drama when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and stories, and talk about parts like rhythm in poems or dialogue in plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including words that affect meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a story, including those that change the feeling or tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific words in a story affect its meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail using specific parts from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme of a story, play, or poem using details from the text and summarize it.

description: Use Historical Analysis and Interpretation to:1. Identify issues and problems of the past. 2. Consider multiple perspectives of various peoples of the past. 3. subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish the challenges indigenous peoples and ethnic and tribal groups around the world have experienced as a result of colonization, imperialism, and assimilation, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difficulties indigenous and ethnic groups have faced because of colonization and forced changes.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how ethnocentrism, stereotypes, xenophobia, and racism impact human rights and social justice of various groups, tribes, and nations around the world, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child understands how unfair ideas like stereotypes and racism affect people's rights and fairness.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the impact the experiences and achievements of individuals and groups from various indigenous, racial, ethnic, tribal, political, and religious backgrounds have had on historical events and current global issues.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people from different backgrounds have influenced history and current world issues.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish key turning points in world history in terms of multiple causes and outcomes.
description_plain: Your child can identify important events in world history and explain their causes and effects.

subject: Social Studies
description: History
description_plain: History

subject: Social Studies
description: Differentiate technological innovation and human-environment interaction in terms of intentional and unintentional consequences, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between new technology and how people affect the environment, including expected and unexpected results.

subject: Social Studies
description: Deconstruct the relationship between geopolitics and demographic shifts in terms of intentional and unintentional consequences, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how politics and population changes are connected and their planned and unplanned effects.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the intentional and unintentional consequences of human-environment interaction.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how people and the environment affect each other, including both intended and unintended results.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish the relationship between movement, technology, and innovation in terms of cultural diffusion on societies around the world, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how movement, technology, and new ideas spread cultures around the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the reasons for and effects of immigration, forced migration, slavery, and settlement on empires, societies, and indigenous populations around the world, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain why people moved, were forced to move, or enslaved and how this affected societies and native peoples.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand how movement has influenced societies now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child understands how movement of people and ideas has changed societies over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Geography
description_plain: Geography

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how economic policies have challenged international interdependence and national and tribal sovereignty in various regions around the world.
description_plain: Your child can explain how economic rules have challenged countries and groups working together and their independence.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare how empires, groups, and nations have used economic decisions and policies to gain or maintain power, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can compare how countries and groups have used economic choices to gain or keep power.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the influence of economic interdependence on the development, interactions, and transformation of empires, societies, nations, and regions, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how countries and empires depend on each other economically and how this has affected their growth and relationships in the past and today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how a desire for resources has impacted the global interactions and economic interdependence of empires, societies, and/or nations, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the desire for resources has influenced how countries and societies interact and depend on each other throughout history and now.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand the economic relationships between groups and nations in terms of power and interdependence.
description_plain: Your child understands how economic connections between groups and countries affect their power and dependence on each other.

subject: Social Studies
description: Economics
description_plain: Economics

subject: Social Studies
description: Critique the effectiveness of cooperative efforts among nations, groups, and international organizations in resolving conflicts and maintaining international stability, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can evaluate how well countries and international groups work together to solve conflicts and keep peace, both now and in the past.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how policies and treaties have led to international conflict, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain policies and agreements between countries have caused conflicts in history and today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Evaluate international diplomacy and the policies of a nation in terms of influence on global conflict and resolutions.
description_plain: Your child can assess how a country's diplomacy and policies affect global conflicts and their solutions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare ways racial, ethnic, and religious groups around the world have demonstrated resistance and resilience to inequities, injustice, and restriction of freedoms, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can compare how different racial, ethnic, and religious groups have resisted unfair treatment and fought for their freedoms over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare various revolutions, rebellions, and movements in terms of motive, consequence, and lasting impact on the freedom and equality of individuals and groups in society.
description_plain: Your child can compare different revolutions and movements by looking at why they happened, what resulted, and how they affected freedom and equality.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish ways in which religious and secular leaders and political systems have used power to sustain, expand, or restrict freedom and equality.
description_plain: Your child can explain how leaders and governments have used their power to limit or expand freedom and equality.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compare ways in which individuals, groups, and governments have gained and maintained power.
description_plain: Your child can compare how people, groups, and governments have gained and kept power.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the relationship between various societies and government in terms of freedom, equality, and power.
description_plain: Your child understands how societies and governments relate to freedom, equality, and power.

subject: Social Studies
description: Civics and Government
description_plain: Civics and Government

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the impact of global interaction on the development of national, tribal, and ethnic identities, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how global interactions have influenced the development of national, tribal, and ethnic identities over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how competing religious, secular, racial, ethnic, and tribal group identities have impacted societies, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how different religious, racial, ethnic, and tribal identities have affected societies in the past and today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain how shared values and beliefs of a culture impact national, tribal, and group identity, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how shared cultural values and beliefs shape the identity of nations, tribes, and groups.

subject: Social Studies
description: Understand the concept of identity in historic and contemporary societies in terms of its development and impacts.
description_plain: Your child understands how identities form and affect societies throughout history and today.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain the impact of scientific and technological innovations on societal change around the world, both now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can explain how scientific and technological inventions have caused changes in societies worldwide, both in the past and now.

subject: Social Studies
description: Deconstruct societies and institutions around the world in terms of the ways in which they were shaped by art, literature, philosophical thought, and religion, now and in the past.
description_plain: Your child can describe how art, literature, philosophy, and religion have influenced societies and institutions over time.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze how artistic, literary, philosophical, technological, and scientific ideas have developed and shaped society and institutions.
description_plain: Your child understands how ideas from art, literature, philosophy, technology, and science have shaped societies and institutions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Behavioral Sciences
description_plain: Behavioral Sciences

subject: Social Studies
description: Taking Informed Action: Generate ideas through which the inquiry facilitates change. ● Devise a plan to enact change based on the results of the inquiry. ● Organize and take individual or collaborative action in order to effect change and inform others.
description_plain: Your child can come up with ideas for change based on research, make a plan, and take action alone or with others to create change and inform people.

subject: Social Studies
description: Communicating Ideas: Construct written, oral, and multimedia arguments. ● Support arguments with evidence and reasoning while considering counterclaims. ● Use proper formatting in citing sources for arguments. ● Develop new understandings of complex historical and current issues through rigorous academic discussions. ● Participate in rigorous academic discussions emphasizing multiple viewpoints in which claims and evidence are acknowledged, critiqued, and built upon in order to create new understandings of complex historical or current issues.
description_plain: Your child can create written, spoken, and multimedia arguments using evidence, consider opposing views, cite sources properly, and participate in discussions to understand complex issues.

subject: Social Studies
description: Developing Claims and Using Evidence: Analyze data from charts, graphs, timelines, and maps. ● Analyze visual, literary, and musical sources. ● Examine change and continuity over time. ● Analyze causes, effects, and correlations. ● Determine the relevance of a source in relation to the compelling and supporting questions.
description_plain: Your child can analyze charts, graphs, timelines, maps, and various sources to understand changes over time, causes and effects, and decide how relevant information is.

subject: Social Studies
description: Gathering and Evaluating Sources: Locate credible primary and secondary sources. ● Identify a variety of primary and secondary sources in support of compelling and supporting questions. Summarize the central ideas and meaning of primary and secondary sources through the use of literacy strategies. ● Determine the origin, context, and bias of primary and secondary sources. ● Differentiate between facts and interpretation of sources. ● Evaluate competing historical narratives and debates among historians.
description_plain: Your child can find trustworthy primary and secondary sources, summarize their main ideas, understand their background and bias, tell facts from opinions, and evaluate different historical viewpoints.

subject: Social Studies
description: Supporting Questions: Identify related issues and problems related to the compelling question. ● Formulate supporting questions.
description_plain: Your child can identify related issues and create supporting questions about a main social studies topic.

subject: Social Studies
description: Compelling Questions: Identify issues and problems in social studies. ● Formulate questions based upon disciplinary concepts.
description_plain: Your child can identify important social studies issues and create questions based on key ideas.

subject: Social Studies
description: The Inquiry Indicators are meant to be used in concert with the content standards in any strand for each grade in the 9-12 grade band. Apply the inquiry models to analyze and evaluate social studies topics and issues in order to communicate conclusions and take informed actions.
description_plain: Your child can use inquiry skills to study social studies topics, analyze information, share conclusions, and take informed actions.
